scores.brief_feedback: Excellent coverage of ecological and economic benefits with strong examples (Bhadla, PM-KUSUM). Minor presentation issues: exceeds word limit (162 vs 150), but content accuracy and example depth are exceptional.

Comprehensive understanding of tsunami formation, triggers, and impacts. Well-structured explanation with excellent examples (2004 Indian Ocean, Krakatoa, Fukushima). Stays within word limit and demonstrates strong conceptual clarity.

Strong analysis of Smart Cities addressing poverty and justice through service delivery and inclusive governance. Acknowledges limitations and safeguards. Examples (PMAY, DAY-NULM) are relevant; critical perspective on "islands of excellence" adds analytical depth.

Excellent articulation of professionalism and nationalistic consciousness in ICS ethos. Strong constitutional references (Articles 14-21, Patel's vision) and concrete examples (Election Commission, DBT). Coherent and well-integrated argument on duty and competence.

Nuanced answer balancing the "aggressive consumerism" critique with evidence of cultural hybridity, knowledge diffusion, and alternative consumption models. Good examples (McAloo Tikki, Fair-Trade, #MeToo) and stays within word limit. Strong analytical balance.

Sophisticated multi-dimensional analysis of Phule's reform work across caste, gender, and class. Excellent use of primary texts (Gulamgiri, Shetkaryacha Asud) and specific examples (Satyashodhak Samaj, widow remarriage home). Slightly exceeds word limit but depth and historical accuracy are exceptional.

Comprehensive coverage of India's consolidation across all four dimensions (polity, economy, education, IR). Rich factual detail (562 princely states, First/Second Plans, UGC 1956, Panchsheel) with clear thematic organization. Strong evidence of integrated understanding of the early independence period.

Well-structured argument on French Revolution's enduring relevance with five distinct analytical angles (democracy, rule of law, nationalism, social justice, caution on excess). Strong examples (Declaration of Rights 1789, Napoleonic Code, Reign of Terror). Analytical depth on contemporary echoes (populism, surveillance) is exemplary.

Excellent geographical explanation of offshore oil distribution linked to plate margins and depositional settings. Specific regional examples (Persian Gulf, Gulf of Mexico, North Sea, Brazil pre-salt) are precise. Clear comparison with onshore reserves regarding trap styles and operational character. Slightly exceeds word limit but technical accuracy is high.

Strong integration of AI, drones, GIS/RS for urban and areal planning with five well-organized use cases. Good contemporary examples (SVAMITVA, PM Gati Shakti, ISRO Bhuvan). Slightly exceeds word limit but demonstrates solid understanding of technology applications in planning. Could deepen on equity/privacy tradeoffs.

Clear explanation of plate tectonics and continental/ocean basin changes via three main boundaries (divergent, convergent, transform). Excellent examples (Pangaea breakup, Tethys closure, East African Rift, Wilson Cycle). Good balance of mechanism, location, and process. Slightly over word limit but conceptually sound.

Well-analyzed distribution of Ganga Basin population linked to physical resources (alluvial plains, soil fertility, water). Good spatial detail (density figures by state, regional variations) and resource connections. Acknowledges constraints (flooding, arsenic). Slightly exceeds word limit but demonstrates strong geographical understanding.

Thoughtful analysis of fast food growth despite health concerns, addressing urbanization, affordability, convenience, and marketing. Indian context (Swiggy, Zomato, platform economy) is well-integrated. Policy awareness (FSSAI, Eat Right India) shows maturity. Slightly exceeds word limit but substantive and nuanced.

Nuanced discussion of conflicts between environmental protection and poor people's needs with concrete examples (protected areas, Narmada dams, Adivasi displacement). Balances critique with rights-based solutions (Forest Rights Act, PESA, MGNREGA). Shows integrated thinking on inclusive vs. exclusionary conservation. Exceeds word limit slightly.

Balanced assessment of tribal development as framed by displacement-rehabilitation axes with specific examples (Sardar Sarovar, Hirakud, Polavaram). Acknowledges incompleteness and advocates rights-based, in-situ development (FRA, PESA, Gram Sabha). Well-structured with clear opinion statement. Slightly under word limit.

Accurate enumeration of corrupt practices under RPA 1951 with proper Section citations. Clear distinction that disproportionate assets alone do not constitute undue influence without demonstrable nexus to voter influence. Good legal framework awareness. Slightly exceeds word limit but substantively strong.

Good explanation of tribunals' necessity and 2021 reforms impact. Correctly identifies benefits (speed, expertise) and concerns (executive influence, independence). References relevant jurisprudence (Madras Bar Association cases, Law Commission). Could be more specific on which tribunals were merged/affected.

Clear comparative analysis of Presidential pardon powers in India vs USA with proper constitutional citations (Articles 72/161, Article II Sec. 2). Explains preemptive pardons concept. Notes India's review limits (Kehar Singh, Epuru Sudhakar) and USA's broader discretion. Exceeds word limit slightly but legally accurate.

Accurate description of J&K Assembly post-2019 reorganization and LG's enhanced powers. Correctly identifies State/Concurrent List jurisdiction with reserved domains (public order, police). References Article 239A framework. Could provide more examples of actual Assembly functioning or legislative outcomes.

Comprehensive coverage of Attorney General's roles, rights, and limitations under Article 76. Correctly notes right of audience, Parliamentary participation without voting, and pleasure-of-President tenure. Good international comparison (UK, USA). Exceeds word limit but substantively rich and well-balanced.
